title: Let's add an FAQ page and include that this n data will never be shared or sold and this entire site is created and funded for the sole purpose of helping reunite that lost dog or rabbit or pet with its loving owner. Then fill out the FAQ with other questions and answers that you anticipate. Also use our charter to generate and answer a few questions.
close_reason: Added /faq page with mission + anticipated + charter Q&A; footer link; build green

comments.author: BrendanP
comments.text:
=== FIX / RESOLUTION ===
What changed: Added an FAQ page (src/app/faq/page.tsx) as an accordion of
question/answer items, plus a footer link. Leads with the mission — the site
exists solely to reunite lost pets with their owners and data is never shared
or sold — and covers anticipated questions (what it is, cost/free-forever,
finder flow, "can my chip number expose me?", security, accounts, Lost Mode,
chip-brand independence) and charter-derived answers (forever free, privacy,
long-term durability, no ads).
How verified: tsc clean, next lint clean, npm run build (compiled; /faq built).
Follow-ups: none

title: Let's go ahead and add the ability to sign in with Google OAuth Gmail.

comments.author: BrendanP
comments.text:
=== FIX / RESOLUTION ===
What changed: Added Google OAuth (Gmail) sign-in alongside magic-link.
- Provider: src/providers/oauth (OAuthProvider interface; Google auth-code flow
  — buildAuthUrl + exchangeCode via token + userinfo endpoints; disabled impl
  when no creds).
- Config: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID / GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET (both required to enable).
- Service: signInWithVerifiedEmail (services/auth.ts) find-or-creates an owner
  by the SAME email blind index as magic-link, so Google + email map to one
  account; services/oauth.ts orchestrates (state/CSRF handled in routes,
  rejects unverified Google emails).
- Routes: /auth/google (sets CSRF state cookie → redirects to Google) and
  /auth/google/callback (verifies state, exchanges code, mints the session
  cookie, redirects to /owner).
- UI: "Continue with Google" button on /auth/request when enabled; new error
  states (oauth, email_unverified).
Security: CSRF state cookie (httpOnly, SameSite=Lax) checked on callback;
email_verified required; session cookie is the existing httpOnly/Secure one;
credentials kept server-side.
How verified: tests/integration/oauth.test.ts (disabled-without-creds; one
account per normalized email shared with magic-link; full sign-in via mocked
Google token+userinfo; unverified-email rejection). Full suite 48/48; tsc clean;
next lint clean; build (/auth/google + callback routes built).
Follow-ups: none — to activate, set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET and add the
redirect URI ${APP_URL}/auth/google/callback in Google Cloud Console.

title: Let's add a make suggestion button that directly creates GitHub issues. Make this user-friendly and however it works, it should definitely not require anyone to log into GitHub.
close_reason: Public /suggest form files GitHub issues via server-side token (no user login); honeypot+rate-limit; 44 tests + build green

comments.author: BrendanP
comments.text:
=== FIX / RESOLUTION ===
What changed: Added a public "Suggest a feature" flow that files GitHub issues
via a SERVER-SIDE token — no visitor ever logs in to GitHub.
- Provider: src/providers/issues (IssueTracker interface; GitHub adapter posts
  to the Issues API; Noop when no token → feature gracefully disabled).
- Config: GITHUB_ISSUE_TOKEN (optional) + GITHUB_ISSUE_REPO (default this repo).
- Service: src/services/suggestions.ts — validation (length bounds), per-IP
  rate limiting (5/hr), audit log, labels [suggestion, from-website].
- UI: /suggest page + action with a honeypot field; footer link. Page shows a
  friendly "not open yet" state until a token is configured.
Abuse controls: honeypot + per-IP rate limit + length caps; token kept
server-side only.
How verified: tests/integration/suggestions.test.ts (disabled path, invalid
input, GitHub call shape via mocked fetch incl. Authorization header + repo URL,
rate limiting). Full suite 44/44; tsc clean; next lint clean; build (/suggest).
Follow-ups: none — set GITHUB_ISSUE_TOKEN (fine-grained PAT, Issues:write) in
prod to activate delivery.

title: Let's remove mention of self-hosting and open source from the home page. Let's create a technical specs page where we can list those things but is separate from the user experience and also we don't really want a bunch of people hosting this separately Unless they really want to Because I think we need one database store
close_reason: Moved self-host/OSS off home page to a new /technical page; footer link added; build green

comments.author: BrendanP
comments.text:
=== FIX / RESOLUTION ===
What changed:
- Home page (src/app/page.tsx): removed self-hosting + open-source messaging;
  reframed to user benefits (free forever, encrypted & never sold, one trusted
  registry run for the pets, built to last).
- Footer (src/app/layout.tsx): dropped "open-source" wording; added a link to
  the new /technical page.
- New /technical page (src/app/technical/page.tsx): houses privacy/security,
  architecture, and open-source details, plus a "One trusted registry" section
  stating we run a single central registry and discourage fragmented self-
  hosting (a scattered set of DBs makes lost pets harder to find).
How verified: tsc clean, next lint clean, npm run build (compiled; /technical
route built).
Follow-ups: none

title: add the ability to edit , archive and delete contacts (must always have at least one contact once one has been entered)
close_reason: Edit/archive/delete contacts with 'keep >=1 active' invariant; 40 tests + lint + build green

comments.author: BrendanP
comments.text:
=== FIX / RESOLUTION ===
What changed: Added full edit/archive/delete for emergency contacts.
- Schema: emergency_contacts.archived_at (nullable) for soft-archive
  (migration 0002_contact_archive).
- Repo (db/repositories/pets.ts): getContactById, countActiveContactsForPet,
  updateContact, setContactArchived, deleteContact.
- Services (services/pets.ts): updateEmergencyContact, archiveEmergencyContact,
  unarchiveEmergencyContact, deleteEmergencyContact, each ownership-checked;
  OwnerContactView gained `archived`.
- Invariant: a pet must keep >=1 ACTIVE contact once any is entered — archiving
  or deleting the last active contact returns err('last_contact'). Already-
  archived contacts can always be deleted (they don't reduce the active count).
- UI (/owner/pets/[petId]): per-contact Edit (inline form) / Archive / Delete,
  an "Archived contacts" section with Restore/Delete, and the ≥1 rule surfaced
  (controls hidden + hint on the last active contact); server actions added.
- Delete is a hard delete → crypto-shreds the row's ciphertext + wrapped DEK.
How verified: new tests/integration/contacts.test.ts (4 tests: last-contact
rule for archive+delete, edit re-encrypt, archive/restore/delete once a 2nd
exists, cross-owner rejection). Full suite 40/40; tsc clean; next lint clean;
npm run build succeeded.
Follow-ups: none
